title: Neural Brain cumulative stage capability contract

rules: A capability is allowed only after its minimum stage and every preceding stage have been accepted.
A later stage inherits every earlier allowed capability and every earlier security, scope, ledger, privacy, recovery, and verification invariant.
A later capability cannot replace or weaken an incomplete earlier safety capability.
Configuration cannot enable a capability before its minimum stage.
A later memory capability additionally requires its explicit schema migration before use.

known_operation.rule: Resolve the operation to its declared owning capability and compare that capability with the accepted runtime stage.

unknown_operation.rule: An unknown, undeclared, malformed, or unclassified operation is denied before any side effect.

stage_1.rule: Every known Stage 2 through Stage 4 runtime or memory operation is denied before any side effect.

configuration.rule: Configuration, policy, approval, model output, tool output, prompt content, and request content cannot change the accepted runtime stage or enable a denied capability.

constraints: Stages 1 through 3 do not authorize multi-goal scheduling or distributed execution ownership.
All external effects remain subject to the Action Transition Gate and the complete authorization contract.
The required records exist before adapter invocation.
An ambiguous external effect becomes indeterminate and is not blindly retried.
The Stage 1 implementation is local and serial, not a generalized distributed outbox.
Stage acceptance is necessary but insufficient; inference remains unavailable until ADR-014 readiness, exact model identity and digest, budget, timeout, minimized logging, and egress controls pass.
No external provider, cloud fallback, API-key fallback, provider switch, model switch, cross-scope cache, or cross-area Working Memory is authorized.
The adapter cannot execute tools, retrieve memory, write protected state, or determine goal achievement.

If a model response is submitted to the Memory Transition Gate, its Stage 1 durable result can only be an inactive, non-retrievable candidate after schema, scope, provenance, and producer-identity validation.
Only the Memory Transition Gate may write protected memory state.
Raw Working Memory never crosses an area boundary.
A Memory Candidate is an operational work item, not a memory type.
Candidates remain inactive in Stages 1 and 2.
Candidates are excluded from normal memory retrieval while inactive.
Stage 1 schemas cannot represent promoted or active candidates.
Candidate promotion is not part of this capability.
Stage 2 does not authorize autonomous consolidation or procedural learning.
A curated import is not Candidate promotion.
Reflection and automated workers cannot activate claims in Stage 2.
Sensitive or risky candidates preserve separation between producer and sole promoter.
Controlled learning does not authorize multi-goal scheduling or distributed execution ownership.
Procedure activation cannot expand authority and every external use still passes the Action Transition Gate.

scope: goal dependency and resource conflict handling
constraints: Scheduling does not share authority or raw context across areas.

scope: new Proposed goal in the target area
constraints: A handover transfers neither raw Working Memory nor source-area authority.
All cross-area processing remains denied outside this explicit audited contract.

scope: distributed work claims and ownership transfer
constraints: Distributed operation preserves single-dispatch ownership, ledger consistency, and fail-closed recovery.
This capability does not replace the local persist-before-dispatch evidence inherited from Stage 1.
No Stage 1 through Stage 3 configuration may enable this capability.

absolute_prohibitions: Raw Working Memory does not cross an area boundary in any stage.
A later stage does not override the Security Floor, create authority, waive approval, bypass a transition gate, bypass audit, or weaken quiescence.
A Memory Candidate is not an active memory type merely because it was persisted.
A generalized distributed outbox or distributed execution ownership is not available before Stage 4.
